thread.section_title: HTDS Content Services is South Asia’s biggest syndication news agency with a vast collection of News, Images and Cartoons. Apart from Hindustan Times and Mint, HTDS Content Services disseminates a wide spectrum of licensed content from more than 200 publications. The content is packaged and syndicated to a broad range of global clients. HTDS Content Services also offers a rare collection of images and cartoons from Hindustan Times Archives
thread.title: Nepal PM invites Elon Musk to visit Nepal
thread.title_full: Nepal PM invites Elon Musk to visit Nepal

title: Nepal PM invites Elon Musk to visit Nepal
text: Kathmandu [[Nepal](/topic/nepal)], November 23 (ANI): [Nepal](/topic/nepal)'s Prime Minister [KP Sharma Oli](/topic/kp-sharma-oli) shared on social media platform X (formerly Twitter) that he recently extended an invitation to Tesla CEO [Elon Musk](/topic/elon-musk) to visit [Nepal](/topic/nepal). "A short while ago, during my conversation with Mr [Elon Musk](/topic/elon-musk), I extended an invitation for him to visit [Nepal](/topic/nepal)," Oli posted. https://x.com/kpsharmaoli/status/1860001064940122161 The announcement came amid growing tensions within [Nepal](/topic/nepal)'s ruling coalition regarding the Belt and Road Initiative ([BRI](/topic/bri)) framework. Earlier on Friday, Oli announced a visit to [China](/topic/china) scheduled for December 2, during which he intends to pursue loan-based projects under the [BRI](/topic/bri) framework. Speaking at a mass gathering in Kathmandu organised by his party, the Communist Party of [Nepal](/topic/nepal)- Unified Marxist Leninist ([CPN-UML](/topic/cpn-uml)), Oli highlighted the potential benefits of Chinese-funded projects despite [Nepal](/topic/nepal)'s current economic challenges. "Under my leadership, a team comprising members of the government will be going to [China](/topic/china). As a Prime Minister, I am embarking on a visit to [China](/topic/china) from 17th Mangsir (December 2) and the visit will be very successful... It's not that am going to [China](/topic/china) to roam around, it is for the benefit of the country," Oli stated. He further emphasised the importance of production-oriented development and reassured the public about the necessity of external [loans](/topic/loans). "As much as possible we don't want to take [loans](/topic/loans) from anywhere; we are not in the condition that we can add on more [loans](/topic/loans) but we have to increase our production. How can we increase our production? It's not something that we need to be depressed about," Oli said. However, coalition partner [Nepal](/topic/nepal)i Congress opposed any new [loans](/topic/loans) under the [BRI](/topic/bri) framework, emphasising the need for [China](/topic/china) to fulfill its existing grant commitments. [Nepal](/topic/nepal)i Congress spokesperson Prakash Sharan Mahat reiterated, "The [Nepal](/topic/nepal)i Congress is not opposed to the [BRI](/topic/bri), we are simply saying that we cannot take on new [loans](/topic/loans) at this time." He added, "If the grant implementation itself is lagging, what is the point of discussing [loans](/topic/loans)? The debt burden is already increasing." Mahat further stressed the importance of addressing ongoing commitments. "The focus should be on implementing the [grants](/topic/grants), especially those under the [BRI](/topic/bri), and avoiding new [loans](/topic/loans)," he stated during a meeting at the Federal Parliament building. (ANI)
